title: Forbidden-APIs clippy lint rule (HBR-QUIET-001/002 surface ban)
goal: Per spec §6.6.6 (AC-QUIET-API-LINT) add a workspace clippy lint that fails the build on any reference to `set_focus`, `show` (on hidden windows), `unminimize`, `AllowSetForegroundWindow`, `AttachThreadInput`, `SetForegroundWindow` (from non-foreground processes) outside `operator_foreground::*`; `LockSetForegroundWindow` is never used.
expected_diff_shape: New clippy.toml at workspace root configuring disallowed-methods; supplementary grep-based fallback check `.GOV/roles_shared/checks/hbr-quiet-api-lint.mjs` for path-scoped enforcement clippy alone cannot express; gov-check umbrella wiring.
unblock_conditions: MT-014 quiet_window + operator_foreground placeholder modules exist so the lint has an allowlist scope.
implementation_notes: Two-layer enforcement because clippy alone cannot scope by file path in stable Rust. Layer 1 (clippy.toml at workspace root): add `disallowed-methods = [ { path = "tauri::Window::set_focus", reason = "HBR-QUIET-001" }, { path = "tauri::Window::show", reason = "HBR-QUIET-001 use QuietWindowBuilder" }, { path = "tauri::Window::unminimize", reason = "HBR-QUIET-001" }, { path = "windows::Win32::UI::WindowsAndMessaging::AllowSetForegroundWindow", reason = "HBR-QUIET-002" }, { path = "windows::Win32::UI::Input::KeyboardAndMouse::AttachThreadInput", reason = "HBR-QUIET-002" }, { path = "windows::Win32::UI::WindowsAndMessaging::SetForegroundWindow", reason = "HBR-QUIET-002" }, { path = "windows::Win32::UI::WindowsAndMessaging::LockSetForegroundWindow", reason = "HBR-QUIET-002 never use" } ]`. Layer 2 (hbr-quiet-api-lint.mjs grep): ripgrep for any of the seven API names in src/ + app/src-tauri/ EXCLUDING `**/operator_foreground/**` and `**/quiet_window.rs`. Any match outside the allowlist is a violation. Output JSONL `{file, line, api_name, reason}`. Exit code 0 pass, 2 fail. Justfile recipe `hbr-quiet-api-lint`, wired into gov-check. Tests: fixture src file outside allowlist calling SetForegroundWindow -> exit 2; same call inside operator_foreground/foreground_exception.rs -> exit 0; clippy lint test: introduce a violation in a test fixture crate and assert cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings fails.
risk_if_missed: Without the lint, any future code path can call SetForegroundWindow and silently violate HBR-QUIET-002 until a focus-audit run catches it post-hoc.
validator_focus: Two-layer enforcement: clippy for method-path-level bans, grep for file-scoped bans; the allowlist (operator_foreground::*, quiet_window::*) is canonical and documented in both layers identically.

builder_handoff_correction_20260525.operator_instruction: Operator clarified that completed/pass-like states were not validator-reviewed and instructed Kernel Builder to mark everything ready for validator handoff except the blocked item.

minimum_controls: Allowlist scope is identical between clippy.toml and the grep script (single source-of-truth comment in both).
Test fixture exercises BOTH layers to prove neither can be bypassed alone.
Lint failure carries the HBR-* rule id in the reason field for traceability.

integration_validator_review.verdict_reason: Two-layer lint exists - clippy.toml bans the builder constructors and .GOV/roles_shared/checks/hbr-quiet-api-lint.mjs scans src/ + app/src-tauri/ for 7 forbidden APIs with the contract's allowlist (operator_foreground/**, quiet_window.rs) - but clippy.toml is missing the 7 method/Win32 paths the MT-017 contract enumerates; those are caught only by the grep layer.

integration_validator_review_prior.verdict_reason: Forbidden-APIs lint is not implemented at either layer: clippy.toml lacks the seven-method disallow list and the path-scoped grep checker script does not exist.
